summary: Tail-recursive scan over i64 indices [0,n) (scan_at) invoking a separate recursive digit-7 counter (count_sevens, recursing on n/10) on indices divisible by 11 or 13; two nested strong-induction proofs composed via a shared aux — count_sevens_correct on descending measure n.toInt.toNat with a log10 digit bound (count_sevens_nat n ≤ k when n < 10^k, hence ≤ 19 for any i64) that discharges the outer accumulator-no-overflow precondition (≤ 19·600 < 2³¹), scan_at_correct on n.toInt.toNat − i.toInt.toNat consumes count_sevens_correct as a witness inside the divisible branch via a Bool-or-to-decide bridge (i % 11 = 0 ∨ i % 13 = 0), partial %? / /? / +? discharged via the Int64.minValue && d=-1 cases and BitVec.saddOverflow / Int.tmod_eq_emod_of_nonneg, with four concrete unit pins closed by native_decide instead of equational unfolding.
